title: tetrapterys crispa subsp. typica
intro: Tetrapterys crispa subsp. typica, also known as the 'Wild Quinine', is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been utilized to treat various health conditions. The plant's leaves have been traditionally used to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat skin conditions, including wounds and burns. Herbal preparations of Tetrapterys crispa subsp. typica include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which have been used to promote overall well-being and provide relief from various health issues.
